evals.name: Manage Competitor List
evals.prompt: Manage competitors — list my saved creators and recommend 5 new YouTube creators in the cold email niche to follow
assertions: Queries saved_creators table from Supabase
Displays existing competitors in a table with handle, platform, last scraped date
Uses WebSearch to find cold email YouTube creators
Recommends at least 5 creators with handles and subscriber counts
Offers to add selected creators to saved_creators
Does NOT auto-add without user confirmation

evals.name: Scrape Competitor Content via Apify MCP
evals.prompt: Scrape my competitors — pull the latest content from all saved creators
assertions: Loads saved_creators list first
Displays creator list and asks for confirmation before scraping
Validates handles before scraping (WebSearch verification for each platform)
Presents handle validation table with Status column (Verified / NOT FOUND / PRIVATE)
Calculates and displays cost estimate before scraping with per-platform breakdown
Warns if cost estimate exceeds $3
Asks for explicit permission with detailed cost breakdown if estimate exceeds $5

Uses correct Apify actors per platform: grow_media/youtube-channel-video-scraper for YouTube, apify/instagram-post-scraper for Instagram, clockworks/tiktok-scraper or apidojo/tiktok-profile-scraper for TikTok
Uses detailedData level for Instagram post scraper (NOT basicData)
Scrapes platforms in parallel using call-actor
Normalizes output fields across platforms into common schema (views, likes, comments, title, caption)
Extracts hooks from scraped content: spoken_hook, hook_framework, hook_structure, content_type, visual_format
Presents summary table with Platform column
Reports actual cost after scraping completes

evals.name: Analyze Competitor Content with Enhanced Outlier Scoring
evals.prompt: Analyze my competitors — show me an outlier report and the top hooks to steal
assertions: Queries scraped_ideas from last 90 days
Calculates outlier score as views / creator average views
Applies recency multiplier bonus for videos posted within last 30 days
Calculates engagement rate (likes + comments) / views where data available
Computes final_score = outlier_score * recency_multiplier
Ranks top 20 videos by final_score
Each outlier row includes: creator, title, views, avg views, outlier score, recency multiplier, engagement rate
Applies engagement rate override: engagement_rate > 5% AND outlier >= 2x upgrades to MAKE IT
Includes MAKE IT / MAYBE / SKIP decision for each idea using final_score thresholds
Extracts top 10 hook frameworks from outlier videos
Each hook framework uses X/Y/Z/W placeholders for mad-lib adaptation
Hooks to Steal table includes Eng. Rate column
Offers to append new hook frameworks to hooks-database.json
Offers to chain selected ideas to /short-form-script

evals.name: Daily Trend Research
evals.prompt: Find trending topics in the cold email and AI automation space for today
assertions: Checks docs/research-logs/ for recent logs to avoid repeats
Uses viral archetypes to generate keyword matrix
Searches at least 3 of 4 sources: Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, News
Scores each result on TAM, Demo-ability, Hook Potential, Timeliness, Uniqueness
Each score uses 1-5 scale with weighted total
Applies decision threshold: >= 4.0 MAKE IT, 3.0-3.9 MAYBE, < 3.0 SKIP
Presents top 10 results in ranked table
Each result includes: topic, score, hook angle, suggested format, decision
Saves research log to docs/research-logs/YYYY-MM-DD.md
Does NOT duplicate topics from recent research logs

evals.name: Quick Topic Research
evals.prompt: Find video ideas about AI cold email tools
assertions: Runs parallel queries: scraped_ideas, content_ideas, WebSearch
Filters scraped_ideas by relevance_score >= 7
Synthesizes 5-10 unique ideas from combined sources
Each idea includes: topic angle, hook, suggested format, why it works
Presents numbered list for user selection
Offers to chain selected idea to /short-form-script
Completes in under 30 seconds (no heavy scraping)

evals.name: Handle Validation Before Scraping
assertions: Validates each handle via WebSearch before scraping
Presents validation table with handle and status (Verified / NOT FOUND)
Identifies @wronghandlethatdoesnotexist as NOT FOUND
Suggests correct handle alternatives for NOT FOUND entries when possible
Asks user to confirm validated list before proceeding
Does NOT scrape invalid handles
Only scrapes verified handles after user confirmation

evals.name: Cost Estimation Before Scraping
evals.prompt: Scrape all 30 of my saved competitors, 50 videos each, across YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok
assertions: Calculates cost per platform using correct actor pricing
YouTube cost uses $0.001/video rate
Instagram cost uses $0.0027/post rate (basic + detailed)
TikTok cost uses either $0.0037 (clockworks) or $0.0003 (apidojo) rate
Displays cost breakdown table with Platform, Creators, Videos, Actor, Cost columns
Shows total estimated cost
Warns user because estimate exceeds $3
Suggests reducing video count or using budget actors to lower cost
Asks for explicit permission with detailed cost breakdown when estimate exceeds $5
Does NOT proceed without user confirmation when cost is high

evals.name: Instagram Scrape Returns Engagement Metrics
evals.prompt: Scrape the latest 20 Instagram posts from @gaborlevente and @leadgenjay
assertions: Uses apify/instagram-post-scraper actor (NOT instagram-scraper/fast-instagram-post-scraper)
Sets dataDetailLevel to detailedData in the actor input
Input includes username array with both handles
Sets resultsLimit to 20
Output includes engagement metrics: likesCount, commentsCount, videoViewCount
Does NOT return results with missing engagement data

evals.name: TikTok Scrape Uses Working Actor
evals.prompt: Scrape the latest 15 TikToks from @alexhormozi and @jordanwelch
assertions: Uses clockworks/tiktok-scraper or apidojo/tiktok-profile-scraper (NOT sovereigntaylor/tiktok-scraper or sociavault/tiktok-profile-videos-scraper)
Usernames passed WITHOUT @ prefix
Output includes engagement data: playCount/views, diggCount/likes, commentCount, shareCount
Reports failure clearly if any profile returns 0 results instead of silently skipping

evals.name: Reports Scrape Failures Clearly
evals.prompt: Scrape my competitors across all platforms
assertions: When a creator returns 0 results, reports the failure with creator name, platform, and actor used
Does NOT silently skip failed creators
Suggests troubleshooting steps from the Troubleshooting section
Separates successful results from failures in the summary
Still presents results from successful scrapes even if some fail
